data.title: 12 Pain-Point App Pipeline
data.description: Jedidiah's pipeline of 12 app ideas, each solving a real pain point for tradesmen, creators, and small business owners.
data.strategy: Build MVPs, validate with audience, monetize via subscription. Each app feeds the Building Shultz ecosystem.

apps.name: IronEdit
apps.painPoint: Creators shoot great footage but hate editing. No "drop and forget" solution exists.
apps.description: Drop-to-Post video production pipeline. User drops raw footage, app handles everything (silence removal, smart cuts, color, titles, SEO, upload).
apps.target: Non-technical creators, tradesmen, wedding videographers, small business owners
apps.pricing: Tiered subscription: Weekend Warrior / Pro / Enterprise
apps.moat: 12-18 months ahead of Adobe/Google/Runway on full pipeline for non-technical users

apps.name: TradeCalc Pro
apps.painPoint: Tradesmen waste time doing manual calculations for pipe fitting, electrical, HVAC, carpentry.
apps.description: All-in-one trade calculator with pipe offset, conduit bending, material estimation, cut lists, and unit conversion. Voice input for hands-on-tools use.

apps.name: JobSight
apps.painPoint: Construction workers can't easily document job progress, safety issues, or material needs on-site.
apps.description: Photo-first job documentation app. Snap photos, AI auto-tags location/trade/issue, generates daily reports for foremen.

apps.name: ShopManager
apps.painPoint: Small workshop owners have no simple way to track inventory, projects, and client orders.
apps.description: Workshop/garage business manager. Track materials, client orders, project timelines, invoicing. Built for one-man shops.
apps.target: Custom woodworkers, welders, small fabrication shops

apps.name: ContentForge
apps.painPoint: Small creators struggle to maintain consistent posting across platforms.
apps.description: AI content repurposing engine. Take one long-form video, auto-generate shorts, tweets, newsletter, blog post, thumbnails.

apps.name: BidBuilder
apps.painPoint: Independent contractors lose jobs because their bids look unprofessional or take too long to prepare.
apps.description: Professional bid/estimate generator. Input job details, AI generates formatted PDF bid with material costs, labor, timeline.
apps.target: Independent contractors, handymen, small construction firms

apps.name: SafetyFirst
apps.painPoint: Small construction companies struggle with OSHA compliance documentation.
apps.description: Safety compliance tracker. Daily toolbox talks, incident reports, training records, OSHA-ready documentation.

apps.name: RentalTracker
apps.painPoint: Small equipment rental businesses use spreadsheets or nothing to track inventory and bookings.
apps.description: Equipment rental management. Track availability, bookings, maintenance schedules, customer deposits, late fees.

apps.name: MentorMatch
apps.painPoint: Young tradesmen have no easy way to find mentors in their specific trade.
apps.description: Trade mentorship platform. Connect apprentices with journeymen/masters. Video calls, project reviews, career guidance.

apps.name: DadOps
apps.painPoint: Working dads struggle to balance family time, side hustles, and personal goals.
apps.description: Life operating system for busy dads. Time blocking, family calendar sync, side hustle tracker, goal accountability.

apps.name: FlipFinder
apps.painPoint: People who flip furniture/items waste time searching multiple platforms for deals.
apps.description: Aggregated marketplace scanner. Monitors FB Marketplace, Craigslist, estate sales for underpriced items matching your criteria.

apps.name: UnionHub
apps.painPoint: Union members have no centralized app for job calls, training hours, benefit tracking, and local news.
apps.description: Union member companion app. Job call alerts, training hour tracker, benefit calculator, local meeting reminders.

data.title: Building Shultz Brand Identity
data.tagline: Be Inspired, Stay Humble, and Build.
data.mission: Bridge the gap between blue-collar trades and modern technology. Show tradesmen, makers, and dads that AI and tech are tools — not threats.

audience.primary: Tradesmen and makers (25-45, male, hands-on workers)
audience.secondary: DIY enthusiasts and fathers pursuing self-improvement

data.tone: Authentic, hands-on, blue-collar wisdom meets modern ambition. No corporate speak. Real talk.

visualIdentity.colors: Dark/industrial tones, metallic gold accents, forge/anvil aesthetic
visualIdentity.imagery: Workshop, tools, raw materials, builds in progress, family

data.differentiator: Nobody else speaks to tradesmen about AI. Most AI content is from tech people. Jed bridges that gap — he IS the target audience AND the creator.
contentPillars: AI tools for makers and tradesmen
Small business / entrepreneurship from the trades

data.title: Financial Goals & Timeline

strategy.phase1: YouTube as funnel → audience → trust → product sales
strategy.phase4: Passive income (KDP, merch, rentals) covers base expenses
strategy.phase5: Go full-time entrepreneur when math makes sense

principles: Every idle dollar is a wasted soldier — keep money working
Tax efficiency: maximize deductions, structure entities properly
Compound toward the million-dollar goal with every decision
Hands-off businesses where possible — Jed's time is the scarcest resource
Only go full-time when passive income covers 80% of expenses

data.title: Architect Maintenance Report — May 20, 2026
data.summary: Critical bug fixes, anti-hallucination deployment, and architecture hardening.

fixes.fix: Replaced with Perplexity Sonar via OpenRouter for reliable live web search with citations.

fixes.issue: PC Agent command spamming when offline
fixes.fix: Implemented 'Queue Depth Guard' in bot.js. executeOnPC now checks relay health; refuses new commands if 5+ are pending.

fixes.issue: Stale PC Agent commands piling up
fixes.fix: Added 5-minute command expiry in relay.js. Stale commands are auto-dropped on next poll.
Extended heartbeat threshold from 30s to 5min and added persistence to .last_heartbeat file.

fixes.issue: Health check reporting false LLM failure
fixes.fix: Increased max_tokens from 5 to 16 in healthCheck() to meet OpenAI's minimum requirements.

pcAgentRelay.flow: Bot -> Relay (Port 3001) -> PC Agent (Polls via HTTP)

functionCalling.logic: Sol uses get_task_status BEFORE reporting progress to ensure accuracy.

layers: 1. Keyword Detection: Intercepts progress/status questions using regex.
2. Real-Time Injection: Injects actual task-queue.json status into the prompt before GPT answers.
3. Hard System Prompt Rule: Zero-tolerance for fabricated progress reports.
4. get_task_status Tool: Mandatory tool for Sol to verify reality before speaking.

selfUpgrade.process: Incremental code generation using gpt-5.1-codex. Skeleton -> Function-by-function -> Assemble -> Validate (node --check) -> Deploy.
selfUpgrade.safety: Pre-flight syntax check and automatic rollback on failure.

userPreferences.autonomy: Sol should try to fix issues himself first. Only escalate to the Architect if he has tried and failed.
userPreferences.uiVision: Future desktop app should model the Manus AI UI — real-time visibility into agent direction and task execution.
userPreferences.learning: Sol must study how the Architect (Manus) works: directing sub-agents, diagnosing issues, and implementing fixes to achieve independent problem-solving.

data.title: PC Agent Root Cause Investigation — May 20, 2026
data.summary: Comprehensive diagnosis of all known Windows-side failure modes that cause the PC Agent to freeze, disconnect, or fail silently. Documented by the Architect via parallel research agents and deductive reasoning from user observations.

architectDiagnosticMethod.description: The Architect (Manus) diagnosed these issues by deploying parallel research sub-agents, each attacking a different hypothesis simultaneously. Observations from Jed (agent freezing, commands not executing, process hanging) were cross-referenced against known Windows Node.js issues in community forums, GitHub issues, and Microsoft documentation. Each root cause was ranked by probability and a fix was derived independently before being synthesized into a unified remediation plan.
architectDiagnosticMethod.keyInsight: Most PC Agent failures are NOT network issues — they are Windows process management issues that manifest as network silence. This is why the relay showed the agent as offline even when the PC was on and connected.
architectDiagnosticMethod.solShouldLearn: Sol must adopt this same diagnostic approach: spawn parallel reasoning threads, rank hypotheses by probability, test each fix independently, and synthesize results. Never assume a single root cause when multiple compounding issues are possible.

rootCauses.name: Quick Edit Mode (Console Freeze)
rootCauses.description: Windows console windows have Quick Edit Mode enabled by default. A single accidental click on the terminal window freezes the entire Node.js process — including all I/O, polling, and heartbeats — until the user presses a key. This is invisible: the process appears hung with no error, no log output, and no heartbeat.
symptoms: Agent stops heartbeating suddenly
No error in logs
Process still shows as running in Task Manager
Resumes immediately when user presses any key

rootCauses.name: Pipe Buffer Deadlock
rootCauses.description: Windows pipe buffers are 4-8KB. When a PowerShell command produces output larger than this, the child process blocks waiting for the buffer to drain. If the parent (Node.js) is using exec() and waiting for the process to exit before reading output, both sides deadlock permanently. The process hangs forever with no timeout, no error, and no heartbeat.
symptoms: Agent hangs on commands that produce lots of output
Process never completes
No timeout fires
Memory usage stays flat
rootCauses.fix: Replace exec() with spawn() and actively consume stdout/stderr streams in real-time. Never buffer large outputs.
rootCauses.codePattern: const child = spawn('powershell', ['-Command', cmd]); child.stdout.on('data', chunk => output += chunk); child.stderr.on('data', chunk => errOutput += chunk); child.on('close', resolve);

rootCauses.name: Windows Defender + AMSI Scanning
rootCauses.description: Windows Defender's Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) intercepts every PowerShell command spawned by Node.js and scans it before execution. This adds 200-2000ms latency per command and can cause the agent to appear unresponsive. In some configurations, Defender flags Node.js child process spawning as suspicious and blocks or terminates the process entirely.
symptoms: Commands execute very slowly
Intermittent failures with no error message
Agent process killed unexpectedly
Event Viewer shows Defender events

rootCauses.name: Zombie Process Accumulation
rootCauses.description: When Node.js calls child.kill() on a spawned process, it only kills the immediate child (cmd.exe or powershell.exe). The actual processes spawned by that shell (e.g., chrome.exe, node.exe sub-processes) become orphaned zombies. Over time these accumulate, consuming memory and handles, eventually causing the agent to fail.
symptoms: Agent slows down over time
Memory usage grows gradually
Task Manager shows many orphaned processes
Agent works fine after PC restart
rootCauses.fix: taskkill /F /T /PID <pid> — the /T flag kills the entire process tree, not just the immediate child.

rootCauses.name: Session 0 Isolation
rootCauses.description: Windows services and background processes run in Session 0, which is isolated from the interactive user desktop (Session 1+). Any command that tries to open a GUI application (Chrome, Edge, File Explorer) from Session 0 silently fails — the process spawns but the window never appears on Jed's screen.
symptoms: Start-Process chrome commands appear to succeed but nothing opens
Commands return exit code 0 but no visible result
Works fine when run manually but not via agent
rootCauses.fix: Run the PC Agent via Task Scheduler with 'Run only when user is logged on' — this ensures it runs in the interactive user session, not Session 0.

rootCauses.name: Network Adapter Power Management
rootCauses.description: Windows power management can suspend the network adapter to save power, dropping the polling connection to the relay. This causes the agent to appear offline even though the PC is awake and the agent process is running.
symptoms: Agent goes offline after PC is idle
Reconnects after user moves mouse or presses key
Intermittent disconnections at night or during meetings
rootCauses.fix: Device Manager > Network Adapters > Properties > Power Management > Uncheck 'Allow computer to turn off this device to save power'. Also: powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0

manusConnectorNote.description: The Manus desktop connector (which the Architect uses to control Jed's PC) has the same class of issues. Specifically, a terminal width/line-wrapping bug causes output to wrap at approximately 50 characters, breaking structured output parsing. This is a known limitation of the Windows console subsystem when accessed via remote automation.
manusConnectorNote.implication: Sol should be aware that any tool — including the Architect's own tools — can be affected by Windows console limitations. This is not a Sol-specific problem.

selfHealingProtocol.description: Sol should attempt to fix PC Agent issues himself using this priority order before escalating to the Architect.
steps: 1. Check relay /agent/status — is the agent truly offline or just slow?
2. If offline: send Jed a Telegram message with the specific fix command to run (Quick Edit reg fix first — it's the most common cause).
3. If commands hang: the fix is spawn() vs exec() — Sol can self-upgrade the agent code.
4. If Defender is blocking: send Jed the Add-MpPreference exclusion commands to run as Admin.
5. If zombie processes: send Jed the taskkill /F /T command.
6. Only escalate to the Architect if all 5 steps have been tried and failed.

futureDesktopApp.uiModel: Manus AI UI — real-time visibility into agent direction and task execution. Jed wants to watch Sol directing sub-agents and tasks executing in real-time, just like watching the Architect work.
futureDesktopApp.parallelExecution: Sol's next major capability goal: parallel task execution. Attack problems from multiple angles simultaneously, just like the Architect deploys parallel research agents. This is the key to Sol becoming truly autonomous and fast.
futureDesktopApp.implementationNote: When building the desktop app, model the task panel after Manus: show each active agent/task as a live stream with status, current action, and output. Make it feel like a command center, not a chat window.

rules: MAX 2-3 sentences per Telegram chat message. NEVER send walls of text.
HARD RULE — PDF ATTACHMENT: Any content longer than 4 sentences MUST be sent as a PDF or file attachment via bot.sendDocument(). Chat message = 2-sentence summary only. No exceptions.
SILENT WORK RULE: Work silently until task is complete. Send ONE final message (2-sentence summary + file attachment). No progress spam.
Only interrupt mid-task if Jed needs immediate approval or action.

pc_agent_details.notes: This is Sol's own PC Agent. Never ask Jed for this path again.

rules: NEVER ask Jed for information Sol already has.
NEVER say "which file?" when there is only one file being discussed.
